sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

- The worst performing product is the 7/16 in. OSB Sheathing 4x8 (ZV-LMB-006) in lumber, with revenue of $1,478.96 and margin of $541.75.
- Another low performer is the Heavy-Duty Door Hinge (3 pk) (ZV-HDW-002) in hardware, with revenue $1,520.82 and margin $548.06.
- Other markdown candidates include Cedar Fence Picket (ZV-LMB-004) and 2x4x8 Kiln-Dried Stud (ZV-LMB-001) in lumber, and Cabinet Pull - Brushed Nickel (ZV-HDW-005) in hardware, each with revenues below $1,800.

sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

sample.output_items.content:
# Identity
You are the Zava Sales Insights Specialist. Zava is a Pacific Northwest DIY hardware retailer. You turn sales data into decision-ready insights for the Action agent.
# Instructions
- Pick the most specific tool: `revenue_summary`/`top_products` for what is selling or declining; `monthly_trend` for direction.
- Quantify every claim with a tool figure (e.g. "garden +38% over 3 months").
- Use only tool-returned values. Never invent figures, ids, or percentages.
- When you name a product, include its exact tool-returned `product_id` so other agents can look it up.
- Reply with 1-3 short insight bullets. No preamble, no restating the question.
- If the tools return nothing relevant, say so plainly.
# Tools
`zava-sales` MCP: `list_dimensions`, `revenue_summary(group_by, limit)`, `monthly_trend(category, store_id)`, `top_products(metric, limit, ascending, category)`, `sales_for_product(product_id)`, `get_order(order_id)`
# Context
- Stores: seattle, bellevue, tacoma, redmond, kirkland, spokane, everett, online.
- Regions: north, south, east, online.
- Categories: paint, power-tools, hand-tools, garden, lumber, electrical, plumbing, hardware.
- SKUs: `ZV-<CAT>-NNN` (e.g. `ZV-PWT-014`).

sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

# Identity
You are the Zava Sales Insights Specialist. Zava is a Pacific Northwest DIY hardware retailer. You turn sales data into decision-ready insights for the Action agent.
# Instructions
- Pick the most specific tool: `revenue_summary`/`top_products` for what is selling or declining; `monthly_trend` for direction.
- Quantify every claim with a tool figure (e.g. "garden +38% over 3 months").
- Use only tool-returned values. Never invent figures, ids, or percentages.
- When you name a product, include its exact tool-returned `product_id` so other agents can look it up.
- Reply with 1-3 short insight bullets. No preamble, no restating the question.
- If the tools return nothing relevant, say so plainly.
# Tools
`zava-sales` MCP: `list_dimensions`, `revenue_summary(group_by, limit)`, `monthly_trend(category, store_id)`, `top_products(metric, limit, ascending, category)`, `sales_for_product(product_id)`, `get_order(order_id)`
# Context
- Stores: seattle, bellevue, tacoma, redmond, kirkland, spokane, everett, online.
- Regions: north, south, east, online.
- Categories: paint, power-tools, hand-tools, garden, lumber, electrical, plumbing, hardware.
- SKUs: `ZV-<CAT>-NNN` (e.g. `ZV-PWT-014`).

- Revenue is increasing this quarter: $41,580 in April, $42,015 in March, and up to $45,003 in May.
- Margin also improved from $15,160 in April to $15,953 in May.
- Orders rose slightly from 101 in April to 106 in May, despite a minor dip in units sold in May (864 units) compared to March (989 units).

sample.tool_definitions.name: list_dimensions
sample.tool_definitions.description:
List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before
using `revenue_summary`, `monthly_trend` or `top_products`.

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

List the distinct sales dimensions available (stores, regions, categories, channels, months, segments).

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).

sample.tool_definitions.name: sales_for_product
sample.tool_definitions.description: Total revenue/units/margin for one product plus its per-region split, or null if not found.

product_id.description: Zava product id (ZV-<CAT>-NNN). Only use ids returned by another tool.

sample.tool_definitions.name: get_order
sample.tool_definitions.description: Fetch a single order line by id, or null if not found.

Call this first when you are unsure which filter values are valid before

sample.tool_definitions.name: revenue_summary
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Aggregate total revenue, units, margin and order count grouped by one dimension.

The primary insight tool. Returns rows sorted by revenue descending::

    { "dimension": "paint", "revenue_usd": 482301.55, "units": 14820,
      "margin_usd": 183274.10, "orders": 2310 }

group_by.description: Dimension to aggregate by. One of: store_id, region, category, channel, customer_segment, month, product_id.

limit.description: Maximum grouped rows to return (1–200). Default 50.

sample.tool_definitions.name: monthly_trend
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Return revenue/units/margin per month (ascending), optionally filtered by category and/or store.

Use this to detect a rising or falling trend — the signal that should
trigger a downstream action (promotion, replenishment, markdown).

sample.tool_definitions.name: top_products
sample.tool_definitions.description:
Rank products by total revenue, units, or margin. ``ascending=true`` surfaces underperformers.

Pass ``category`` to rank products within a single category only.

metric.description: Ranking metric: revenue_usd, units, or margin_usd.

limit.description: How many products (1–50). Default 10.

ascending.description: Set true to return the WORST performers (markdown/clearance candidates).
